candidates.why: A standalone operating-level depreciation line appears above Operating income and outside the selected COGS row 'Vessel operating expenses'. It is not shown as a child/component nested within that selected row, so it appears to be a separate operating D&A amount that should be added separately later. In this shipping-company context, the standalone depreciation line most plausibly relates to operating vessels rather than SG&A.

variables.notes: OK. Selected the standalone operating depreciation row for COGS_DA because it sits separately from the chosen COGS bucket ('Vessel operating expenses') and there is no more granular COGS-specific D&A row. Not assigned to XSGA_DA to avoid double counting.

candidates.why: Although this is a standalone operating depreciation row, nothing in the excerpt indicates that it is SG&A-specific or a component of 'General and administrative expenses'. Using it for XSGA_DA would risk double counting if it is primarily vessel/operating depreciation.

variables.notes: No explicit SG&A-specific depreciation/amortisation row is shown. The generic standalone 'Depreciation' line is more plausibly operating/COGS-related than embedded in or attributable to the selected XSGA bucket 'General and administrative expenses'.
